ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Maɣe Yesu i Betlehem ɔmagɛ̃ ame i Yudea ku ɔwi gɔ ame i Herodes to ɔɖe sigara. Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, manɔgbadze ma nɣe ara i awãmi iso ɔbɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ maba Yerusalem Ɔmagɛ̃ ame.
translated: When Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea during the reign of King Herod, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem asking, "Where is the newborn king of the Jews? For we have seen His star in the east and have come to worship Him."

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔkpi ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame i Egipte.
translated: Herod ordered Joseph to take the child to Egypt to hide him.

text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.
translated: Is the river's eye also a place where we fish? Minyɔ went there to retrieve the basket. The fish caught is now in the pot.

text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.

text: Ne Petro ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Lokã sɔ ɔnukuare nto loɣɛ! Ɣaa si ɔkpadzɛ̃ me kutsue si iiɖe gbaã iɖe sɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ!” Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, kɔkɔ ɔɖi kanya.

text: Ɣɛɛ mi wũ mito mite sɔ si ɔrere ba ira nɛ ikote sɔ ɔ̃asu ɔbuai ɔ̃ maɣese fiɛ ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Nnɛ loasu lotã-ɔ loɔsu lotã Ɣaa.’ ne, iisinya sɔ ɔturi aawarɛ ɔ̃ ɔse ɣee ɔ̃ ɔnyi.
translated: "Therefore, I know day and night that this story is given to ask for the path, in the way of the ɔ̃asu and ɔbuai, to do well, as it is said, 'We take the lotã path, the loɔsu lotã path, Ɣaa.' And then, speaking again, returning again, to see and come now."

translated: Jesus said to them, "I have come to save the world, the lost sheep, so that they may return to my Father's house."

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Gɔ Pilato ɔnya sɔ ɔ̃ibawo ma adzuni ɔfinikira ne kulu to kuaba ne, ɔsu ndu ɔfore nrɔɔ̃ i iturikuri katɔ̃ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Wũ kɔrɔ̃ gu kukpa na i ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe kukpi ame. Mi itɔ̃me iɖe.”
translated: Pilato led his followers to the place where they were crucified and killed, and as he stood there, he said to them, "You have chosen the path of the cross, but I will follow my own way. I will go my own path."

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Be ɔso ato akarɛ me itɔ̃me i ira sɛɛ iso? Ɔturi ɔwɛ̃ saã pɛ nɖe ɔsɛɛ, fiɛ si ato abie ngbã mɛ loisɛ miro ne, mɔɛ̃ Ɣaa mmara ɔɖuɖu la.”
translated: And Jesus said to the crowd, "Why do you quarrel among yourselves because you have no understanding? Is it not written in your law, 'I said, You are gods'? If He called them 'gods' to whom the word of God came—and the Scripture cannot be broken—do you say of Him whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, 'You are blaspheming,' because I said, 'I am the Son of God'?"'
